metadata.summary: Implement cloud-native backup with AWS Backup (vault, plan, cross-region), S3 lifecycle rules (IA/Glacier/Deep Archive), object lock for immutability, and Terraform IaC for the full stack.

content.title: Cloud-Native Backup with AWS Backup and S3
content.summary: Implement AWS-native backup using AWS Backup service (vaults, plans, cross-region copy rules) for managed resources (RDS, DynamoDB, EBS, EFS), and S3 lifecycle policies for object storage tiering. Enable S3 Object Lock for immutable ransomware-resistant backups. Provision the full stack as Terraform IaC.

why.p: AWS Backup provides a centralized control plane for backup operations across RDS, DynamoDB, EBS, EFS, S3, EC2, and FSx — replacing fragmented per-service backup configurations with a single policy layer. Cross-region copy rules address the "same-storage-system snapshot" failure mode by ensuring a copy exists in a separate AWS region. Vault Lock (or WORM policies) prevents deletion even by account administrators, satisfying compliance requirements for immutable backup storage.

S3 lifecycle rules automate the cost-tiering of backup objects: fresh backups stay in Standard for fast restores; older backups transition to Standard-IA (30d), Glacier (90d), and Deep Archive (365d). Without lifecycle rules, all backup objects stay in Standard and the storage bill grows linearly with retention. With well-tuned lifecycle rules, storage cost drops by 70-90% for long-retention policies.

list.item: AWS-hosted workloads with RDS, DynamoDB, EBS, or EFS resources that need centralized backup policy management.
Compliance requirements mandating cross-region backup copies and immutable retention periods.
Cost optimization for existing backup buckets where objects never leave Standard storage class.
Ransomware protection requiring object lock (WORM) on backup storage.
Consolidating fragmented per-service backup scripts into a single Terraform-managed AWS Backup plan.
On-premises or non-AWS workloads — use Restic, BorgBackup, or Velero with self-managed object storage instead.
Simple S3-bucket-to-bucket replication where S3 native replication (CRR) is sufficient and AWS Backup overhead is not justified.
Kubernetes workload backup — use Velero which understands K8s resource state; AWS Backup only covers underlying EBS/EFS volumes.

section.name: s3-lifecycle
section.p: S3 lifecycle rules transition objects through storage classes based on age. The canonical tiering for backup buckets: Standard (0-30d, hot restores), Standard-IA (30-90d, infrequent restores), Glacier Instant Retrieval (90-365d, occasional DR), Deep Archive (365d+, compliance cold storage). Enable versioning to protect against accidental deletes; add noncurrent version transitions to expire old versions on the same schedule.

rule.statement: S3 backup buckets MUST have versioning enabled, public access blocked on all four dimensions (block_public_acls, block_public_policy, ignore_public_acls, restrict_public_buckets), server-side encryption with KMS, and at least one lifecycle rule transitioning objects to a cheaper storage class after 30 days.
rule.rationale: A public backup bucket is a data breach. An unencrypted bucket fails most compliance audits. A bucket without lifecycle rules grows unbounded and inflates storage costs. These four settings are the minimum viable backup bucket configuration.

example.title: AWS CLI: configure S3 bucket lifecycle

section.name: terraform-aws-backup
section.p: Terraform modules for AWS Backup: vault (primary + DR region), plan (daily/weekly/monthly rules with lifecycle and cross-region copy), selection (tag-based resource targeting), IAM role, and KMS key. The DR vault in a separate region receives copies via the copy_action rule — this is the critical step that makes the backup survive a regional outage.

example.title: Terraform: AWS Backup vault, plan, and cross-region copy
Terraform: S3 backup bucket with lifecycle, encryption, object lock

rule.statement: Backup buckets containing critical data MUST enable S3 Object Lock in GOVERNANCE or COMPLIANCE mode with a minimum 30-day retention period. The IAM policy for the daily-runner account MUST NOT include `s3:DeleteObject`, `s3:DeleteObjectVersion`, or `s3:PutBucketObjectLockConfiguration` — only a separate break-glass account may hold those permissions.
rule.rationale: Immutable object lock prevents ransomware and rogue scripts from deleting backup objects. GOVERNANCE mode allows an account administrator to override (useful for testing); COMPLIANCE mode prevents any override including by root. Air-gapping the delete permission ensures the daily-runner account cannot be used to destroy backups even if compromised.

antipattern.description: Enabling Object Lock + S3 lifecycle policies without testing their interaction — a lifecycle rule can delete objects that Object Lock should protect.
antipattern.reason: Object Lock and lifecycle rules interact: a lifecycle noncurrent_version_expiration rule can attempt to delete locked versions. AWS will reject the delete request but may silently skip it, leaving locked but orphaned versions that consume storage. Always test on a non-prod bucket with short retention values before applying to production.

checklist.check: Create AWS Backup vault with KMS encryption key.
Create DR region vault in a separate AWS region.
Define backup plan with daily, weekly, and monthly rules.
Add copy_action to each rule pointing to the DR region vault.
Configure lifecycle policies: warm (30d IA), cold (90d Glacier), archive (365d Deep Archive).
Set up resource selection by tags (Backup=true) or explicit ARN list.
Apply selection_tag to all RDS, DynamoDB, EFS, EBS resources that should be backed up.
Enable S3 bucket versioning on all backup buckets.
Block all public access on backup buckets — all four settings.
Configure MFA delete for critical buckets (root account required).
Enable Object Lock in GOVERNANCE mode with 30-day minimum retention for ransomware protection.
Test restore from vault in DR region to verify cross-region copy is functional.
Test that Object Lock actually prevents deletion on a non-prod bucket.
Air-gap credentials: daily-runner lacks s3:DeleteObject; break-glass account holds it in a separate vault.

antipattern.description: Agent generates AWS Backup resource selection without selection_tag, causing the plan to match zero resources silently (no error, no backups).
antipattern.reason: AWS Backup selection with no tag filter and no explicit resources list matches nothing. Always validate the selection produces at least one resource in a test plan before relying on it for production.

antipattern.description: Agent generates cross-account restore IAM policy missing `kms:Decrypt` on the source key in the target account, producing a restore that fails with AccessDenied on the encrypted recovery point.
antipattern.reason: Cross-account restores need the target account's role to assume the source KMS key's trust policy. Agents output happy-path policies that miss this KMS trust. Always validate restore in the target account, not just write success in the source.

antipattern.description: Agent sets up S3 Object Lock + lifecycle noncurrent_version_expiration without testing their interaction on a non-prod bucket, relying on logic alone.
antipattern.reason: Object Lock and lifecycle policies interact in non-obvious ways. A lifecycle rule attempting to delete a locked version is silently skipped by S3, which can leave orphaned locked versions consuming unbounded storage. Always test on a non-prod bucket with short retention values (minutes/hours) before applying to production.

section.name: llm-prompts
section.prompt:
Generate Terraform configuration for AWS Backup:
Resources to backup: - RDS instances: [list or tag-based] - DynamoDB tables: [list or tag-based] - EBS volumes: [list or tag-based] - EFS filesystems: [list or tag-based]
Policy: - Daily backup at: [time UTC] - Weekly backup on: [day] - Monthly backup on: [day] - Cross-region copy to: [region] - Lifecycle: [days to cold storage, days to delete]
Generate: 1. Backup vault with KMS encryption 2. DR region vault 3. Backup plan with all rules 4. Resource selection by tags 5. IAM roles and policies 6. SNS notifications

Analyze and optimize backup storage costs:
Current usage: - Storage backend: [S3/GCS/Azure Blob] - Current size: [TB] - Monthly growth: [%] - Current cost: [$] - Retention: [policy]
Analyze: 1. Current cost breakdown by tier 2. Lifecycle optimization opportunities 3. Deduplication potential 4. Compression improvements 5. Retention policy review 6. Alternative storage classes 7. Projected costs with recommendations
